content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the branch against 808707cede105036103429ccad825cf9faddd8f6, read the changed source/test paths, and spot-checked the new create-from-bead coverage under `nix develop`. The main same-bead/orphan checks look consistent with the prompt, but there is one medium correctness gap: legacy runless active lineage is still silently ignored at create time.

## Amendments

1. finding_summary: `project create-from-bead` still permits a parallel project when the only existing active lineage row is a legacy `run_id: null` attempt. The new read-only helper explicitly drops every non-terminal row without a run ID (`src/adapters/fs.rs:5089-5104`), `execute_create_from_bead` relies on that helper for its duplicate guard (`src/cli/project.rs:587-597`), and the integration test now locks that behavior in as success (`tests/cli.rs:13549-13594`). In an upgraded workspace with one of these legacy active rows, create-time validation will silently ignore the existing same-bead attempt, mint a second project, and leave the ambiguity to be "repaired" only later on a start-time write. That misses the bead's stated goal of surfacing or blocking inconsistent active lineage at create time instead of allowing another task to be created.
remediation_hint: Treat non-terminal runless rows as an inconsistent/orphaned active lineage that blocks creation with a repair hint, or run the same legacy-row repair before duplicate detection so create-time behavior matches start-time behavior.
   Rationale: This only affects legacy/corrupt lineage states, but those are exactly the states the new hardening is supposed to make explicit. As shipped, operators can still double-claim a bead during project creation in those workspaces.
